title: The Threshold

features.name: the light-line
features.desc-on-touch: a sharp boundary where the dusk above ends and the understory below begins. You stand at the line. One foot in visibility. One foot in a darkness that has learned to keep knowledge without light.

features.name: the fungal archive
features.desc-on-touch: mushrooms bracket by bracket, stepping downward into the shade, each one a smaller version of itself, each one holding spores that remember older forests. The fungus doesn't see. It knows.

features.name: the root-written page
features.desc-on-touch: carved in living wood below your sight line. The roots have made their own writing here—patterns of growth and scarring that read like a text no one taught you. The understory's book, still growing its own words.

features.name: the library of quiet
features.desc-on-touch: spaces between roots where nothing needs to speak. The knowledge the understory keeps is stored in the grammar of growing together without a common language. You can almost read it if you stop listening.

inscription: here the light remembers itself, and the darkness begins its own archive; neither one can erase what the other has witnessed

file.name: Understory_Plant_Taxonomy_Handwritten_Marginalia_Cross_Referenced_As_Prayer.pdf
file.note: Victorian botanical survey with later annotations in pencil margins, then in pen, then in bark-scratch. Each layer refuses to erase the one before. Where taxonomy ends, the understory's own names begi

exits.label: back up into the library where the trees still remember bark and root
deeper into the understory where the last threshold is not written down
sideways along the border where understory and archive learn to read one another
